name: Houghton Park and Play
description:
The Houghton Park and Ride was purchased by the City of Kirkland in early 2024 for recreational use. Temporary recreation improvements have been added to transform the site into the Houghton Park and Play while the City evaluates long-term uses of the site.

Parking is available on the site and there are limited restroom facilities. Please enter the site from NE 70th place.

There are four temporary pickleball courts at this location.

    The two eastern courts (closest to parking) are challenge courts. If both challenge courts are full and there are players waiting, limit play to one game to 11 points and rotate.
    The two western courts (closest to 116th Ave and the gardens) are timed courts. If both timed courts are full and there are players waiting, limit play to 30 minutes and rotate.

The courts are available on a first-come, first served basis from 8AM to dusk.

name: Jennings Park
description: Jennings Park Pickleball courts are open play. There is a board for Paddle stacking when there are more players than courts, in which, paddles will go into the board from left to right. If two paddles in, when a game finishes, 2 players come off a court, two go in. You can decide which two or winners stay and split. If 3 paddles, 3 off, 3 on, if 4 paddles, 4 off, 4 on, etc.. Or, if you are drilling or have your own group and chose not to rotate players in, you can play on the courts for 30 minutes, then you must come off to allow others waiting opportunity to get on. Your paddles will also go back into the paddle stack and must wait your turn to get back onto the court. At this time there is no differentiating level of play, you play with who you get.
